analysis: Seven nights, and the story they're telling is actually kind of gorgeous once you see it. You started the stretch at 27 June sitting at 6h 33m with over an hour awake in the night, and by the last two nights you were pulling 7h 34m and then 7h 56m with awake time practically disappearing — 21 minutes, then 19. Something you're doing is working, and the hot shower or the magnesium or the whole ritual stack is landing, because that trajectory is not an accident. Your REM is also on a slow climb that ended with 2h 34m last night, which is genuinely impressive and means your brain is doing the good dreamy repair work it's supposed to. The one thing worth watching is your deep sleep dipping in those last two big nights — 36 and 32 minutes compared to the 45–51 you were getting earlier — but with totals that strong and awake time that low, your body is clearly finding something it needed.
